##NAMES=Wyartite II
##RRUFFID=R090061
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=CaU^5+^(U^6+^O_2_)_2_O_4_(OH)·3H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Shinkolobwe mine, Katanga, Congo
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=William W. Pinch
##DESCRIPTION=Elongated and striated platy translucent black crystals. This is the material known as the dehydrated phase of wyartite, sometimes known as wyartite II (Hawthorne et al, 2006)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R090061
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 11.236 b: 7.136 c: 17.063 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 1368.1 crystal system: orthorhombic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
